title: Mismatch between documentation and REST API
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


/kind feature

**Description**

When looking at the REST API documentation
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/system-(compat)/operation/SystemInfo

In top right corner I see `GET /info`
and I can do a curl request like

```
curl --unix-socket /Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock 'http:///d/info'
```
it works

now if I go to the 'libpod info' page https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/system/operation/SystemInfoLibpod

in top right corner it says `GET /libpod/info`

but if I do a curl on `/libpod/info` as written it fails
```
curl --unix-socket /Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock 'http:///d/libpod/info'
```
I have `Not found` error

using `v4.2.0` prefix it works

```
curl --unix-socket /Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock 'http:///d/v4.2.0/libpod/info'
```

Funny thing, I can use random numbers like `v5.0.0` and it's still working but without prefix it doesn't work
```
curl --unix-socket /Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock 'http:///d/v5.0.0/libpod/info'
```

**Describe the results you received:**

not working without prefixing /libpod/ with a version scheme

**Describe the results you expected:**

working without prefixing with a version (expect to have alias to the latest API)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
macOS with brew 4.2.0 
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Seems like a bug in the handler, the version number should not be required for the info endpoint
I tried also pods and events, same issue (when using `/libpod`) without versioning
oh that is terrible ... @mheon remember you asking that question about needed stuff built for a release? :)

Knew I forgot something

On Thu, Aug 18, 2022 at 11:24 Brent Baude ***@***.***> wrote:

> oh that is terrible ... @mheon <https://github.com/mheon> remember you
> asking that question about needed stuff built for a release? :)
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15376#issuecomment-1219629302>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCCEMZ5YTYT7JQCLV53VZZIRXANCNFSM565OPWZQ>
> .
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

The largest challenge in fixing this issue is the current tooling generates the documentation from a swagger 2.0 definition parsed from code comments. Our toolchain allows one [BasePath](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/server/doc.go#L46) not the four the API service exposes. Those being:

- unversion compatible, added to support old tools
- versioned compatible, current tools
- versioned libpod, tools wishing to use libpod enhancements
- unversioned libpod, /_ping only

I would add text to [pkg/api/server/doc.go](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/cdae6d18277996f063697fd605f62a9650727fc9/pkg/api/server/doc.go) explaining when to use the different BasePath's. Changing the BasePath to /vX.Y.Z/libpod implies doc.go becomes a file to update during releases and all the compatible endpoints will be rendered with .../libpod which is not correct either. Swagger 3.0 allows multiple server URLs to be defined which could be a way out. But the existing toolchain is not moving in that direction https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/1122

@mheon The /_ping endpoint is available on all  BasePath's, a client can use the results from that operation to build the URL's for other operations. An unversioned /info is problematic as to what the response should be over releases. /_ping is very stripped down (headers only, "OK" payload) and the same handler for all four endpoints.

/cc @baude
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think it's still an issue but I'll check
